question_text: A piece of dry ice (solid carbon dioxide) at -78°C is left in a warm room. It gradually disappears without becoming a liquid. Name this process and explain why it occurs.
correct_answer: This is sublimation. Dry ice sublimes because the solid structure has weak bonds, so particles can escape directly to the gas phase when heated without melting to a liquid first.
explanation: Sublimation is the process where a solid turns directly into a gas without passing through the liquid phase. Dry ice has very weak intermolecular forces, making this transition possible at room temperature.
